step_by_step_thinking:
Finasteride directly decreases the size of the prostate through its mechanism of action involving inhibition of 5alpha-reductase enzymes, resulting in decreased production of DHT. This leads to a reduction in prostatic tissue volume.
\boxed{C} Based on the detailed evidence showing how finasteride blocks the intraprostatic conversion of testosterone to DHT and subsequently reduces prostatic tissue volume, making it the clear candidate to decrease the size of the prostate.
Reference:
[article-18212_67], [article-18212_66], [article-16992_6], etc.
raw_response:
Finasteride directly decreases the size of the prostate through its mechanism of action involving inhibition of 5alpha-reductase enzymes, resulting in decreased production of DHT. This leads to a reduction in prostatic tissue volume.
\boxed{C} Based on the detailed evidence showing how finasteride blocks the intraprostatic conversion of testosterone to DHT and subsequently reduces prostatic tissue volume, making it the clear candidate to decrease the size of the prostate.
Reference:
[article-18212_67], [article-18212_66], [article-16992_6], etc. \boxed{C}
